fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Connecticut Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Connecticut - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity connecticut, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to connecticut, respiratory therapist compact connecticut, connecticut respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Connecticut DPH — Respiratory Care
board.mailing_address:
Connecticut Department of Public Health
Practitioner Licensing & Investigations Section
410 Capitol Ave., MS #12HLS
P.O. Box 340308
Hartford, CT 06134

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Connecticut.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Visit the eLicense System at https://elicense.ct.gov/ and create a new user account by following the registration prompts.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the eLicense System, select the 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' application, and complete all required sections of the online application form.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the $200 application fee directly through the eLicense System using a credit card or other accepted payment method.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Request your official academic transcripts from your accredited respiratory care program to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Connecticut Department of Public Health.

steps.title: Verify NBRC Credentials
steps.description: Arrange for the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) to send official verification of your Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT) or Certified Respiratory Therapist (CRT) credentials directly to the Connecticut Department of Public Health.

steps.title: Submit License Verifications (Endorsement)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, request official verification of all respiratory care licenses you have ever held from each state's licensing authority, sent directly to the Connecticut Department of Public Health.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Connecticut respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Connecticut. To work in Connecticut, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Connecticut while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Connecticut
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Connecticut, create an account and submit your application through the eLicense System.
